title: Bosses
subtitle: Poise, Stagger, and the sizing law

content.text: Large-asset engagement doctrine. Read before quoting a price.
A boss is not a bigger stat block — it is sized. The chassis and roles live on Enemy Framework; this page owns the boss numbers: Poise, action economy, the mandatory kit, and the sizing law.
Poise & Stagger
Bosses carry a Poise track: 8 standard, 12 apex. Hard control — pins, grapples, Trigger Locks, Rattles, forced movement — deals 1–2 Poise damage instead of applying its effect.

content.label: STAGGER — at Poise 0
content.text: Until the end of the next round:
items: The boss loses its reaction suite.
Attacks against it gain +1 crit beat (beats — see Graze & Crits).

content.text: Then Poise resets 2 higher. Control builds aren't blanked against bosses — their verbs convert into the party's burst window.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Which control effects deal 1 Poise damage and which deal 2 is unprinted — the docs print the range only.
Action economy
A standard boss takes 2 activations per round; an apex takes 3 — interleaved with the party in the normal alternation (see Turn, AP & Reactions).
The mandatory kit
Every boss kit must include three pieces. This is the floor — no exceptions:
One no-roll zone attack — damage that never consults Evasion.
One contested grab — resolved as a contested Check, not an attack roll.
One arrival-keyed zone — the effect applies on entering or appearing in the zone, not on crossing a line. Teleports arrive, so teleports pay the toll: Rift Step included.
The sizing law

content.label: THE SIZING LAW
items: Shell phases cap at Value 4, with at least one stagger window per phase.
Phase HP = party size × 10 × target rounds for mid tables; × 15 for veteran tables.
Tune boss outgoing damage to ~3–4 HP per party member per round through mid armor (benchmark: 13 per round across a four-player table).
Budget one stolen action per fight (Override: ACT — see Influence).
At the veteran multiplier, assume a buffed, scar-suppressed party.

content.text: A boss shell is a doctrine check; parties that scouted brought openers. A Value 5 shell is a seven-round wall for an unprepared mid party and a 2.4-round speed bump for a doctrine party — never print that spread on a fight the intel layer had no chance to close (see Intel).
Sizing against the top of the curve
The player-side peaks and the budget line that answers each. The player rules live on their own pages: Dual Wield & Titan Grip, Fury, Sin Eater, Patterns, Fortress, Influence.

rows: Dual-Titan + scar-Fury damage (~70 party DPR at the top end)
The veteran multiplier: Phase HP × 15 sizes phases against it, not surprised by it.
The mandatory no-roll zone attack and contested grab — neither consults Evasion.
Assume buffed, scar-suppressed parties whenever the veteran multiplier applies.
Arrival-keyed zones — appearing inside triggers them.
One stolen boss action per fight is pre-budgeted into the action economy.

content.text: Worked boss: Broodmother Crawler

entries.label: Body
entries.value: Shell 4/3/80 over Flesh 120 — sized for a four-player mid table at ~3 rounds per phase.

entries.label: Kit
entries.value: Sweep — no-roll, Adjacent cluster, 3d10. Grab-and-Press — contested; grabbed targets take automatic punch-through each round. Burrow Surge — repositions; her arrival zone Rattles (arrival-keyed; Rattled — see Conditions). Brood calls — 2 Char Hound hordes arrive on a clock.

entries.label: Phase 1
entries.value: Shell intact. Weak-point stagger windows. Carve memory: the crack location prices the chitin (see Carving).

entries.label: Phase 2
entries.value: Cracked frenzy: +1 activation, Sweep widens, and her Break line appears — she retreats below 20% to save the brood. Even monsters run arithmetic.

content.text: Worked boss: Pacification Detail

entries.label: Core
entries.value: Sentinel Field Officer — Elite+ chassis, Poise 8, 2 activations — plus a Riot Unit wall and guard hordes (see Bestiary).

entries.label: Kit
entries.value: Calls — marks and repositions the formation; kill the voice and the formation is meat. Suppression order — zone, no-roll (Suppressed — see Conditions).

entries.label: Poise engine
entries.value: The officer's Poise regenerates 2 per round while any Riot Unit stands. The retinue is the poise pool; the fight is a disassembly puzzle.

entries.label: Break
entries.value: Pure doctrine — the Detail withdraws, in order, the moment projected losses exceed the contract line. You don't rout corps; you re-price them.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Two unquantified lines in the worked bosses: how much the Broodmother's Sweep widens in phase 2, and what “Elite+ chassis” adds over the Elite row for the Field Officer. The docs print neither.
Reclassification from “engagement” to “large asset” entitles the field team to revised hazard pay. It does not entitle the asset to revised behavior. Plan the windows.
